log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 2100-2400 geng road [24pln-00356]: recommendation on site and design to demolish four existing commercial b
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 2100-2400 Geng Road [24PLN-00356]: Recommendation on Site and Design to Demolish Four Existing Commercial Buildings and Construct 65 Three-Story Buildings Containing 145 For Sale Townhome Units. Thirteen Percent (19) of the Units Would Be Deed Restricted to Low Income Households. The Project Also Includes a Conditional Use Permit for the Residential Use and a Vesting Tentative Map to Merge Two Parcels and Re-Subdivide For Condominium Purposes Which Council will Consider In Conjunction with the Site and Design Application. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on July 8, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt from CEQA in Accordance with CEQA Guidelines Section 15183 (Comprehensive Plan Consistency). Zoning District: ROLM (E)(D)(AD) -Research, Office and Limited Manufacturing (Embarcadero) with Site and Design and Automobile Dealership Combining Districts.
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish commercial buildings and build 145 townhomes on Geng Road.

log.key: public hearing / quasi-judicial. 3781 el camino real [24pln-00161]: recommendation on a major architectural review application to demolish c
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING / QUASI-JUDICIAL. 3781 El Camino Real [24PLN-00161]: Recommendation on a Major Architectural Review Application to Demolish Commercial and Residential Buildings Located at 3727-3737 & 3773-3783 El Camino Real, 378-400 Madeline Court, and 388 Curtner Avenue, and to Construct a Seven-Story, Multi-Family Residential Housing Development Project with 183 Units, 13 Percent of Which Would be Provided at a Rate Affordable to Low Income. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with Builder’s Remedy (California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5)). A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed on February 15, 2024. CEQA Status: Exempt Under AB 130 (Public Resources Code section 21080.66). Zoning Districts: CN (Commercial Neighborhood) and RM-30 (Multi-Family Residential).
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish buildings and build a 183-unit residential project with affordable units.

log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'reviews funding' but the official item indicates the body is bringing this forward for 'Approval of funds' — a stronger action than merely reviewing. log.key: santa clara county science and engineering fair association 2026 science fair winners from palo alto
log.title: Santa Clara County Science and Engineering Fair Association 2026 Science Fair Winners from Palo Alto
log.draft: The City Council reviews Palo Alto's 2026 Science Fair winners from Santa Clara County.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline adds interpretive language not present in the official item. The item states the agenda topic is about 'Science Fair Winners from Palo Alto' but does not explicitly state that 'the City Council reviews' them. The verb 'reviews' interprets the Council's action beyond what the agenda item itself confirms. A neutral headline should mirror the official item more directly (e.g., 'Santa Clara County Science and Engineering Fair Association 2026 Science Fair Winners from Palo Alto').

log.key: endorsement of the connect bay area transit initiative public comment
log.title: Endorsement of the Connect Bay Area Transit Initiative Public Comment
log.draft: The City Council considers endorsement of the Connect Bay Area Transit Initiative.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states the City Council 'considers endorsement' of the initiative, but the official agenda item only identifies it as a 'Public Comment' agenda item. There is no explicit statement in the provided text that the Council is considering endorsement—only that public comment on the initiative is scheduled. The headline adds an interpretation of the item's purpose that is not directly grounded in the official text provided.

log.key: quasi-judicial. 4103 old trace road [25pln-00296]: request for a streamlined housing development review to construct 16 dwelling units on ei
log.title: QUASI-JUDICIAL. 4103 Old Trace Road [25PLN-00296]: Request for a Streamlined Housing Development Review to Construct 16 Dwelling Units on Eight Total Lots, Including Eight Single-Family Residences and Eight Junior Accessory Dwelling Units. One Single Family Home and JADU would be Provided at Below Market Rate. A Separate Vesting Tentative Map Application (25PLN-00298) Has Been Filed for the Proposed Subdivision. The Project Requests Concessions and Waivers Pursuant to State Density Bonus Law. A Compliant Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application Was Filed on October 1, 2025. CEQA Status: Exempt Pursuant to Assembly Bill 130 (Public Resources Code section 21080.66). Zone District: RE (Residential Estate).
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal for 16 housing units on Old Trace Road.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states 'The Architectural Review Board reviews' but the official item identifies the body as '(body: Architectural Review Board)' in a quasi-judicial context listing it as an agenda item. The headline's framing of 'reviews' could be interpreted as understating the quasi-judicial/decision-making nature of the proceeding. Additionally, simplifying '16 Dwelling Units on Eight Total Lots, Including Eight Single-Family Residences and Eight Junior Accessory Dwelling Units' to merely '16 housing units' omits material complexity about the project type that residents on different sides of housing debates would consider important to the nature of the proposal.
